description: VP Core Networks, Head of Managed Services, and Enterprise CISO must review the architecture.

name: Architecture reviews must be updated concurrently with major OEM firmware capabi
description: Architecture reviews must be updated concurrently with major OEM firmware capability releases or yearly network infrastructure refreshes.

name: Designing a 99
description: Designing a 99.99% Tier-1 SLA topology requires the customer to provide dual, distinct power phases backed by independent UPS units on-site.

name: P1 Threat (Exploitation/Active Breach) must be acknowledged within 15 minutes
description: P1 Threat (Exploitation/Active Breach) must be acknowledged within 15 minutes.

name: P1 Threat containment (network isolation) must be initiated within <4 hours
description: P1 Threat containment (network isolation) must be initiated within <4 hours.

name: P2 Event (Elevated Risk/DDoS Attempt) must be acknowledged within 30 minutes
description: P2 Event (Elevated Risk/DDoS Attempt) must be acknowledged within 30 minutes.

name: P2 Event resolution tracking must occur within <8 hours
description: P2 Event resolution tracking must occur within <8 hours.

name: NGFW capabilities must include granular URL filtering (60+ active categorical de
description: NGFW capabilities must include granular URL filtering (60+ active categorical defenses), DPI, and stateful IPS engines.

name: NGFW throughput per appliance must range dynamically between 200 Mbps to 1 Gbps
description: NGFW throughput per appliance must range dynamically between 200 Mbps to 1 Gbps.

name: NGFW latency profiles must not degrade below <50ms failover paths
description: NGFW latency profiles must not degrade below <50ms failover paths.

name: SIEM and Threat Intelligence Log Correlation must be implemented as a technical
description: SIEM and Threat Intelligence Log Correlation must be implemented as a technical capability.
evidence: 3.3 SIEM and Threat Intelligence Log Correlation

name: Standard multitenant cloud SIEM architectures accept upward of 5000+ Events Per
description: Standard multitenant cloud SIEM architectures accept upward of 5000+ Events Per Second (EPS) without requiring bespoke hardware node expansion.

name: Active commercial feed aggregation (CrowdStrike, Recorded Future) provides immed
description: Active commercial feed aggregation (CrowdStrike, Recorded Future) provides immediate SIEM IOC correlation matching across the domestic backbone.

name: The Data Privacy Officer owns the DPDP Alignment Policy
description: The Data Privacy Officer owns the DPDP Alignment Policy.

name: The Lead SOC Architect owns the SOC Infrastructure Design
description: The Lead SOC Architect owns the SOC Infrastructure Design.

name: Containment rigorously entails blocking hostile traffic from laterally progressi
description: Containment rigorously entails blocking hostile traffic from laterally progressing across the managed Wide Area Network.

name: VI Business (India) does not assume responsibility for sanitizing malware direct
description: VI Business (India) does not assume responsibility for sanitizing malware directly off customer-owned server clusters or manipulating standalone end-user compute devices unless an explicit Endpoint Detection (EDR) commercial scope is integrated.

name: VI Business operates dedicated hyperscaler Meet-Me-Rooms (MMR) anchored primaril
description: VI Business operates dedicated hyperscaler Meet-Me-Rooms (MMR) anchored primarily within NESCO IT Park, Mumbai DC, and Chennai DC to bypass standard ISP latency variables.

name: The Nationwide RAN Map must be stored in the SharePoint/VIB_Network_Topology rep
description: The Nationwide RAN Map must be stored in the SharePoint/VIB_Network_Topology repository as VI_LTE_NLD_Footprint.kml.

name: The Nationwide RAN Map must be stored in the SharePoint/VIB_Network_Topology rep
description: The Nationwide RAN Map must be stored in the SharePoint/VIB_Network_Topology repository as VI_LTE_NLD_Footprint.kml.

name: The owner of the policy is the VP, Core Networks
description: The owner of the policy is the VP, Core Networks.

name: The reviewers of the policy are the VP Engineering and Enterprise CISO
description: The reviewers of the policy are the VP Engineering and Enterprise CISO.

name: The policy must be reviewed bi-annually
description: The policy must be reviewed bi-annually.

name: Hardware replacement in remote Tier-3 locations is constrained by proximity to d
description: Hardware replacement in remote Tier-3 locations is constrained by proximity to designated regional engineering depots mapped during the site survey phase.

name: Global cross-border international links (e
description: Global cross-border international links (e.g., Mumbai to London IPLC) are excluded from this standard domestic policy and operate under distinct contractual rules and international carrier SLAs.

name: The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix provides an authoritative schedule of service le
description: The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix provides an authoritative schedule of service level commitments, including uptime, latency, MTTR, and incident reaction boundaries.

name: The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix serves as the absolute ceiling for operational g
description: The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix serves as the absolute ceiling for operational guarantees made during competitive bidding.

name: The intended audience for this document is Service Managers, Commercial Analysts
description: The intended audience for this document is Service Managers, Commercial Analysts, and RFP Writers.

name: The document is applicable for responding securely to RFP Service Level Annexure
description: The document is applicable for responding securely to RFP Service Level Annexures with quantitative targets backed by financial penalty frameworks.

name: MTTR is defined as Mean Time to Repair / Resolve
description: MTTR is defined as Mean Time to Repair / Resolve.

name: P1/P2/P3/P4 priority incident levels map to ITIL v4 incident models
description: P1/P2/P3/P4 priority incident levels map to ITIL v4 incident models.

name: Promising any SLA target superior to those defined in Section 3
description: Promising any SLA target superior to those defined in Section 3.1 requires authorization from the VP Managed Services Operations and Regional CFO.

name: Tier-1 Hub (Dual Link Active/Active) has a 99
description: Tier-1 Hub (Dual Link Active/Active) has a 99.99% monthly SLA target.

name: Tier-1 Hub SLA measurement requires redundant power / HA Chassis
description: Tier-1 Hub SLA measurement requires redundant power / HA Chassis.

name: Tier-2/3 Branch (Active/Standby ZTP) has a 99
description: Tier-2/3 Branch (Active/Standby ZTP) has a 99.95% monthly SLA target.

name: Tier-2/3 Branch SLA is measured at the SD-WAN CPE edge
description: Tier-2/3 Branch SLA is measured at the SD-WAN CPE edge.

name: SD-WAN Link Failover Event must execute stateful session preservation with a thr
description: SD-WAN Link Failover Event must execute stateful session preservation with a threshold of <50ms per event.

name: ExpressRoute Latency (NESCO to Azure) must be <8ms round trip, based on 95th per
description: ExpressRoute Latency (NESCO to Azure) must be <8ms round trip, based on 95th percentile polling.

name: Direct Connect Port Availability must be 99
description: Direct Connect Port Availability must be 99.9% monthly.

name: P1 (Critical) incidents (core systemic failure, active network breach, ransomwar
description: P1 (Critical) incidents (core systemic failure, active network breach, ransomware pivot) require 15-minute acknowledgment and containment within 4 hours.
evidence: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

name: P2 (High) incidents (degradation of critical HA link, DDoS attempt, elevated SIE
description: P2 (High) incidents (degradation of critical HA link, DDoS attempt, elevated SIEM alert) require 30-minute acknowledgment and resolution within 8 hours.
evidence: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

name: P3 (Medium) incidents (intermittent jitter/latency, localized performance issues
description: P3 (Medium) incidents (intermittent jitter/latency, localized performance issues) require 2-hour acknowledgment and resolution within 24 hours.
evidence: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

name: P4 (Low) incidents (standard service requests, policy changes, routine queries)
description: P4 (Low) incidents (standard service requests, policy changes, routine queries) require 4-hour acknowledgment and resolution within 3 days.
evidence: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

name: P1 (Critical) incident updates must be provided every 1 hour
description: P1 (Critical) incident updates must be provided every 1 hour.

name: P2 (High) incident updates must be provided every 2 hours
description: P2 (High) incident updates must be provided every 2 hours.

name: P3 (Medium) incident updates must be provided every 8 hours
description: P3 (Medium) incident updates must be provided every 8 hours.

name: P4 (Low) incident updates must be provided daily
description: P4 (Low) incident updates must be provided daily.

name: The company must define and maintain a Metric Target for service performance
description: The company must define and maintain a Metric Target for service performance.

name: An Enforcement Engine must be implemented to monitor and enforce service complia
description: An Enforcement Engine must be implemented to monitor and enforce service compliance.

name: Service reports must be generated in the specified formats, including Monthly Se
description: Service reports must be generated in the specified formats, including Monthly Service PDF.

name: Network Availability must be monitored and reported through the Overlay Orchestr
description: Network Availability must be monitored and reported through the Overlay Orchestrator Dashboard.

name: ITSM ServiceNow Log Engine must be utilized for logging service incidents
description: ITSM ServiceNow Log Engine must be utilized for logging service incidents.

name: SLA Exception Reports must be generated to document service deviations
description: SLA Exception Reports must be generated to document service deviations.

name: Service interruptions caused by mass regional power grid collapses, global hyper
description: Service interruptions caused by mass regional power grid collapses, global hyperscaler outages, or customer-side infrastructure vandalism automatically suspend MTTR measurements.

name: Deviations from standard OpEx modeling require Vice President level corporate fi
description: Deviations from standard OpEx modeling require Vice President level corporate finance authorization when selling core hardware (CapEx) instead of leasing it.

name: Monthly billing commences strictly 24 hours after validation of a site's Zero-To
description: Monthly billing commences strictly 24 hours after validation of a site's Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) profile load and formal User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

name: Deal Authorization DOAs are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Delegation_Of_Auth
description: Deal Authorization DOAs are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Delegation_Of_Authority.pdf and owned by Corporate Finance.

name: Hardware Break-Fix Fees are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Depreciation_Engin
description: Hardware Break-Fix Fees are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Depreciation_Engine.xlsx and owned by Supply Chain Finance.

name: Accepting a Customer's MSA draft without injecting standard protective terms req
description: Accepting a Customer's MSA draft without injecting standard protective terms requires VP Corporate Finance and General Counsel authorization.

name: The sole and exclusive remedy for SLA metric failures is the issuance of Service
description: The sole and exclusive remedy for SLA metric failures is the issuance of Service Credits.
evidence: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages

name: Accumulated Service Credits may not exceed 10% of the Monthly Recurring Charge (
description: Accumulated Service Credits may not exceed 10% of the Monthly Recurring Charge (MRC) for the affected site or service in the month of failure.
evidence: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages

name: The Baseline MSA Text is governed by the General Counsel, and the DoT Compliance
description: The Baseline MSA Text is governed by the General Counsel, and the DoT Compliance Annex is governed by the VP, Regulatory Affairs.

name: Exceptions to the Liability Cap or SLA Penalty Caps require escalation to the De
description: Exceptions to the Liability Cap or SLA Penalty Caps require escalation to the Deal Desk as documented in the Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide [VIB-LEGAL-002].

name: SLA measurement methodologies mapping to commercial penalties are defined in the
description: SLA measurement methodologies mapping to commercial penalties are defined in the SLA and KPI Standard Matrix [VIB-COM-005].
